title: Assembly of Multiple-Address Letters
plain_language_rule: Figure 8-4 shows a suggested way to assemble a multiple-address letter for signature and mailing. The manual does not prescribe a specific text-based assembly order in section 8-4 — the assembly procedure is illustrated in the figure. Figure 8-4 shows a suggested package including brief sheet if required, original/letters to be signed, envelopes or mailing labels if required, information-addressee copies with enclosures, official file copy with enclosures, and background material. Figure 8-4 shows signature page, enclosures, and background material may be tabbed. Figure 8-4 shows checking or arrowing the intended addressee on each copy. Figure 8-4 shows exposing the left margin of the official file copy for initials and dates.
source_text: 8-4 Assembly of Multiple-Address Letters. Figure 8-4 shows a suggested way to assemble a multiple-address letter for signature and mailing.
notes: This section refers entirely to Figure 8-4 for guidance. The manual uses the word 'suggested,' indicating advisory tone. No prescriptive assembly order is stated in the text. This is a mailroom-level procedure not amenable to automated validation. Figure 8-4 is rule-bearing but uses suggested assembly guidance. Keep this rule procedural/advisory. Do not convert the suggested assembly order into a hard validator requirement. No renderer behavior is implied.
